CT.regulatory_body: Connecticut Department of Consumer Protection (DCP), Gaming Division
CT.launch_notes: Retail launched Sept 30, 2021 at Foxwoods/Mohegan Sun. Online launched Oct 19, 2021.
CT.archive_depth: From October 2021 to present (full history in CSV)
CT.requires_js_notes: Data available via direct CSV download from data.ct.gov API - no JS needed
operators: MPI Master Wagering License CT, LLC
CT.retail_online_split_notes: Data is for ONLINE sports wagering only. Retail data from tribal casinos may be reported separately under tribal compacts.

data_fields.name: Licensee
data_fields.description: Name of the licensed operator entity

data_fields.name: Fiscal Year
data_fields.description: State fiscal year (e.g., 2025/26)

data_fields.name: Month Ending
data_fields.description: Last day of the reporting month

data_fields.name: Wagers
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered (handle)

data_fields.name: Patron Winnings
data_fields.description: Total amount paid out to winning bettors

data_fields.name: Cancelled Wagers
data_fields.description: Wagers that were cancelled/voided

data_fields.name: Monthly Resettlements (3)
data_fields.description: Adjustments to previously settled wagers

data_fields.name: Online Sports Wagering Win/(Loss)
data_fields.description: Net win or loss from sports wagering before taxes

data_fields.name: Federal Excise Tax (4)
data_fields.description: Federal excise tax at 0.25% of net wagers

data_fields.name: Unadjusted Monthly Gaming Revenue
data_fields.description: Gaming revenue before promotional deductions

data_fields.name: Promotional Coupons or Credits Wagered (5)
data_fields.description: Total promotional credits used by patrons

data_fields.name: Promotional Deduction (6)
data_fields.description: Amount deducted from GGR for promotional play

data_fields.name: Total Gross Gaming Revenue
data_fields.description: Final GGR after all deductions

data_fields.name: Payment (7)
data_fields.description: Tax payment to state (13.75% of Total GGR)

data_fields.name: Notes
data_fields.description: Any amendments or corrections noted

CT.ggr_definition: Online Sports Wagering Win/(Loss) minus Federal Excise Tax, minus Promotional Deductions
CT.ggr_formula: GGR = Wagers - Patron Winnings - Cancelled Wagers + Resettlements - Federal Excise Tax - Promotional Deduction
CT.promo_deduction: Yes - Promotional Coupons/Credits are deducted from GGR
CT.tax_basis: Gross Gaming Revenue (after promotional deductions and federal excise tax)
CT.tribal_compact_notes: CT operates under tribal compacts with Mashantucket Pequot (Foxwoods/DraftKings) and Mohegan Tribe (Mohegan Sun/FanDuel). CT Lottery Corp operates Fanatics.
CT.scraping_approach: Direct CSV download from data.ct.gov API endpoint. No scraping needed - structured data available via API. URL: https://data.ct.gov/api/views/xf6g-659c/rows.csv?accessType=DOWNLOAD. Also supports JSON, XML formats.
CT.notes: Excellent data source. CT publishes structured CSV via data.ct.gov with full operator breakdown, all financial fields, and complete history since Oct 2021. 3 operators only (DraftKings, FanDuel, Fanatics). Tribal compact model. Online-only data in this dataset; retail tribal data may be separate.
CT.log: Fetched portal.ct.gov page - found DCP gaming division links. Found data.ct.gov CSV endpoint via web search. Downloaded 160-line CSV with all historical data. All 3 operators clearly identified with all financial columns.

KY.regulatory_body: Kentucky Horse Racing and Gaming Commission (KHRC)
KY.launch_notes: Sports wagering launched September 28, 2023 (online and retail simultaneously).
KY.archive_depth: From September 2023 to present. First report covers Sept-Dec 2023.
KY.requires_js_notes: Primary data access via Tableau dashboard requires JavaScript. PDF reports also available for download.
KY.operator_notes: KY uses a venue/service provider pairing model. Each licensed venue has a designated online and retail service provider. Penn Sports is marketed as ESPNbet.
KY.retail_online_split_notes: Reports have separate Online and Retail sections with full breakdowns for each.

data_fields.name: Name of Operator / Service Provider
data_fields.description: Venue name paired with service provider brand

data_fields.name: Handle
data_fields.description: Total amount wagered for the reporting period, including future bets

data_fields.name: Wagers Settled
data_fields.description: Both wagers paid out and wagers resolved for the reporting period

data_fields.name: Winnings
data_fields.description: Amount returned to winning players for the reporting period

data_fields.name: Adjusted Gross Revenue
data_fields.description: Sum of settled wagers collected less winnings paid and excise taxes paid according to federal law

data_fields.name: Kentucky Excise Tax (Online)
data_fields.description: State tax on AGR at 14.25% for online wagers

data_fields.name: Kentucky Excise Tax (Retail)
data_fields.description: State tax on AGR at 9.75% for retail wagers

KY.ggr_definition: Adjusted Gross Revenue (AGR) = Settled Wagers Collected - Winnings Paid - Federal Excise Taxes
KY.ggr_formula: AGR = Wagers Settled - Winnings - Federal Excise Tax
KY.promo_deduction: Not explicitly shown in reports - appears to be factored into AGR calculation
KY.scraping_approach: PDF download and parsing. The Tableau dashboard may offer API access if its public URL can be reverse-engineered. PDF reports have structured tables that can be parsed with tabula or camelot. Each page covers one month with Online and Retail sections.
KY.notes: KY launched Sept 2023. Uses venue/service provider pairing model unique among states. Tableau dashboard is primary reporting mechanism but PDF is downloadable. Reports are structured with FY cumulative + individual month pages. 7 online operators and 7 retail venues. Different tax rates for online (14.25%) vs retail (9.75%).
KY.log: Fetched KHRC page - found Tableau dashboard and PDF links. Downloaded 2023 PDF report (5 pages covering Sept-Dec 2023 data). All operator/venue pairings confirmed from PDF. Glossary definitions extracted from report footer.

TN.launch_notes: Tennessee was the first US state to launch online-only sports betting (no retail). First operators went live Nov 1, 2020.
TN.primary_format: PDF (CSV available from ~2025 onwards, some 2024 months)
TN.archive_depth: From November 2020 to present (PDF all months; CSV from late 2023/2024 onwards)
TN.requires_js_notes: Direct file downloads via predictable URL patterns. Page itself uses standard HTML.
TN.operator_breakdown_notes: TN does NOT break down data by operator. Only statewide aggregate totals are reported.
operators: Hard Rock Bet (Seminole Hard Rock Digital, LLC)
TN.operator_notes: Operators are listed on tn.gov/swac/licensees-registrants.html but revenue is NOT broken down by operator in the reports. Up to 12 licensees historically.
TN.retail_online_split_notes: Tennessee is ONLINE ONLY - no retail sportsbooks exist. No split needed.

data_fields.name: Gross Wagers
data_fields.description: Total amount of all wagers placed during the period

data_fields.name: Adjustments
data_fields.description: Wager adjustments (voids, cancellations, resettlements)

data_fields.name: Gross Handle
data_fields.description: Gross Wagers minus Adjustments - the net handle subject to taxation

data_fields.name: Privilege Tax Assessed
data_fields.description: 1.85% tax calculated on Gross Handle

TN.ggr_definition: TENNESSEE DOES NOT REPORT GGR. Tennessee taxes HANDLE, not GGR. The state switched from 20% GGR tax to 1.85% handle tax in July 2023.
TN.ggr_formula: N/A - Tennessee does not calculate or report GGR. Tax = Gross Handle * 1.85%
TN.tax_history: Pre-July 2023: 20% tax on adjusted gross revenue. Post-July 2023: 1.85% privilege tax on gross handle.
TN.promo_deduction: N/A under handle-based tax. Previously allowed under GGR model.
TN.tax_basis: Gross Handle (total wagers minus adjustments) - UNIQUE among US states
TN.scraping_approach: CSV files available with predictable URL patterns for recent months. PDF files for older months can be parsed but contain minimal data (only 4 fields). CSV structure is simple but uses non-standard formatting (quoted field names with bullet characters). Direct download, no authentication needed.
TN.notes: CRITICAL: TN is unique - taxes HANDLE not GGR since July 2023. Reports are extremely simple - just 4 aggregate fields (Gross Wagers, Adjustments, Gross Handle, Privilege Tax). No operator breakdown, no sport breakdown, no revenue/GGR data. Tennessee was first US state to be online-only. CSV files have inconsistent URL naming patterns across years. Limited data utility compared to other states.
TN.log: Fetched tn.gov/swac/reports.html - found PDF and CSV links organized by year. Downloaded Feb 2025 PDF and Feb 2026 CSV. PDF confirms minimal data: just gross wagers, adjustments, handle, and privilege tax. CSV has same 4 fields in a non-standard CSV format. URL patterns vary slightly between years (some include '(CSV)' suffix, others don't).

LA.regulatory_body: Louisiana Gaming Control Board (LGCB) / Louisiana State Police (LSP) Gaming Enforcement Division
LA.url_original_status: truncated/broken - full URL at url field above
LA.launch_notes: Retail sports betting launched Oct 7, 2021. Mobile/online launched Jan 28, 2022. Available in 55 of 64 parishes (voter referendum).
LA.primary_format: Excel (XLSX) - both mobile and retail reports available in Excel
LA.archive_depth: From FY 2021-2022 (July 2022) to present. Older data in archive section.
LA.requires_js_notes: Direct file downloads. Page uses accordion-style expandable sections but files are direct links.
LA.operator_breakdown_notes: The statewide mobile report shows AGGREGATE statewide data, NOT per-operator. However, the data is broken down by SPORT TYPE. Individual operator data is not publicly reported.
operators: American Wagering, Inc. d/b/a Caesars Sportsbook
BetFair Interactive US, LLC d/b/a FanDuel Sportsbook
DK Crown Holdings, Inc. d/b/a DraftKings
RSI LA, LLC d/b/a BetRivers Sportsbook
Penn Sports Interactive, LLC d/b/a theScore Bet (formerly ESPN Bet)
FBG Enterprises Opco, LLC d/b/a Fanatics Sportsbook
Bally's Interactive, LLC d/b/a Bally Bet
LA.operator_notes: Operators are licensed but individual operator revenue is NOT published in the monthly reports. Only aggregate mobile and retail statewide totals.
LA.retail_online_split_notes: Separate reports for 'Sportsbook - Mobile' and 'Sportsbook - Retail'. Each is a separate file (PDF and Excel).
LA.parish_breakdown_notes: Retail reports break down by individual casino/sportsbook location which maps to parishes. Mobile reports are statewide aggregate. 55 of 64 parishes allow sports betting.

data_fields.name: Month
data_fields.description: Reporting month (e.g., Jul-25, Aug-25)

data_fields.name: Wagers vs Previous Year
data_fields.description: Percentage change in wagers compared to same month prior year

data_fields.name: Wagers Written
data_fields.description: Total amount of wagers written/placed during the month

data_fields.name: Promo Deduct.
data_fields.description: Promotional wager deductions subtracted from gross proceeds

data_fields.name: Net Proceeds
data_fields.description: Net gaming proceeds after payouts and promo deductions

data_fields.name: Taxes Paid
data_fields.description: State taxes paid by operators

data_fields.name: Win %
data_fields.description: Win percentage (Net Proceeds / Wagers Written)

data_fields.name: Net Proceeds vs Previous Year
data_fields.description: Percentage change in net proceeds vs same month prior year

data_fields.name: Baseball (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from baseball wagers

data_fields.name: Basketball (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from basketball wagers

data_fields.name: Football (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from football wagers

data_fields.name: Soccer (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from soccer wagers

data_fields.name: Parlay (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from parlay wagers

data_fields.name: Other (Net Proceeds)
data_fields.description: Net proceeds from all other sport/event types

LA.data_fields_notes: Mobile report includes monthly breakdown, FY cumulative, calendar year cumulative, and all-time totals. Sport breakdown has footnote: 'promo wager deduction may not be included in by-sport figures'.
LA.ggr_definition: Net Proceeds = Wagers Written - Payouts - Promotional Deductions
LA.ggr_formula: Net Proceeds = Wagers Written - Payouts - Promo Deductions. Taxes calculated on Net Proceeds.
LA.promo_deduction: Yes - Promotional deductions are subtracted and shown as a separate column
LA.tax_rate_history: Online: 15% until July 2025, then 21.5% from Aug 2025. Retail: 10% throughout.
LA.tax_basis: Net Proceeds (Gross Revenue after promo deductions)
LA.tax_notes: Due to state law allowing losses incurred by operators to offset future net proceeds, actual tax payments may not calculate to the tax rate.
LA.scraping_approach: Excel (XLSX) files available for direct download. URL pattern requires looking up media IDs from the page (not predictable). Best approach: scrape the gaming-revenue-reports page to extract all download links, then download XLSX files. Parse Excel for structured data. Both mobile and retail reports in separate files.
LA.notes: Louisiana has EXCELLENT data quality: sport-by-sport breakdown (unique among tier 3 states), YoY comparisons, promotional deduction tracking, win percentages, and multi-year history in a single report. Reports are prepared by LSP Gaming Audit. Parish-level breakdown available in retail reports. Mobile reports are statewide aggregate. Tax rate recently increased to 21.5% online. Excel format makes parsing straightforward.
LA.log: Original URL was truncated. Found correct URL at lsp.org gaming-revenue-reports page. Found both PDF and Excel formats for mobile and retail sportsbook data. Downloaded Feb 2026 mobile PDF, mobile XLSX, and retail XLSX. PDF confirmed rich data: monthly breakdown, FY/CY cumulative, sport-by-sport split, YoY comparisons. Archive page available for historical data.

NC.regulatory_body: North Carolina State Lottery Commission (NCSLC) / NC Gaming Commission
NC.url_status: working (reports page may render as image; /reports/ alternate URL has proper links)
NC.launch_notes: Online sports wagering launched at noon on March 11, 2024. Online only - no retail sportsbooks.
NC.requires_js_notes: Direct PDF links on page. Some rendering may use JS for styling but links are in HTML.
NC.operator_breakdown_notes: Monthly reports show AGGREGATE statewide data only. No per-operator breakdown in published reports.
NC.operator_notes: 8 licensed interactive sports wagering operators. Operator list available at ncgaming.gov/approved-licensees/ but revenue is not broken down by operator.
NC.retail_online_split_notes: North Carolina is ONLINE ONLY for sports wagering. No retail sportsbooks (tribal casinos have separate tribal gaming not in these reports).

data_fields.name: Month
data_fields.description: Calendar month of reporting period

data_fields.name: Paid Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: The amount of wagers made by players using real money

data_fields.name: Promo Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: The amount of promotional funds used by players

data_fields.name: Total Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: Paid wagers + promotional wagers combined

data_fields.name: Cancelled/Void Wagers
data_fields.description: Wagers invalidated due to event changes or errors

data_fields.name: Amounts Paid as Winnings
data_fields.description: Total winnings paid out to players

data_fields.name: Gross Wagering Revenue
data_fields.description: Total amounts received from wagers less amounts paid as winnings, before deductions

data_fields.name: Estimated Tax Proceeds
data_fields.description: Estimated state tax at 18% on Gross Wagering Revenue

NC.ggr_definition: Gross Wagering Revenue = Total Wagering Revenue - Cancelled/Void Wagers - Amounts Paid as Winnings
NC.ggr_formula: GWR = (Paid Wagering Revenue + Promo Wagering Revenue) - Cancelled/Void Wagers - Amounts Paid as Winnings
NC.promo_deduction: Promos are INCLUDED in handle (Total Wagering Revenue includes both paid and promo wagers). Promo wagering revenue is tracked separately but NOT deducted from GGR.
NC.tax_notes: NC does NOT allow promotional deductions from GGR for tax purposes. Promo wagers are tracked but tax is on full GGR.
NC.scraping_approach: PDF download with predictable URL pattern. PDF contains a single well-structured table per fiscal year showing monthly rows. Can be parsed with tabula/camelot. Page 2 contains definitions/glossary. FY-based reporting (July-June).
NC.notes: NC launched March 2024 - relatively new market. Reports are clean single-table PDFs organized by fiscal year. 7 data columns per month. Includes both paid and promotional wagering as separate columns - good for analysis. No operator or sport breakdown. 8 licensed operators. Online only. Reports include footnotes for resettlements. Glossary/definitions included on page 2 of report.
NC.log: Fetched ncgaming.gov/about/reports - page rendered as image (Adobe Express). Used alternate URL ncgaming.gov/reports/ which had proper HTML links. Downloaded Mar 2026 PDF report. PDF has FY2026 data (July 2025 - Feb 2026) on page 1 with 7 columns, and FY2025 definitions on page 2. Clean tabular format confirmed.

KS.launch_notes: Soft launch Sept 1, 2022 (Gov. placed first bet). Full launch Sept 8, 2022.
KS.archive_depth: From January 2023 to present (Sept-Dec 2022 may be available separately)
KS.requires_js_notes: Direct PDF links on page. Standard HTML with PDF download links.
KS.operator_notes: KS uses casino/provider pairing model. State owns 4 casinos, each can host up to 3 online skins. Reports show Casino + Provider in separate columns. Note: Bet 365 showed $0 revenue in Feb 2026.
KS.retail_online_split_notes: Reports have Subtotal - Retail and Subtotal - Online sections. Both monthly and fiscal year summaries provided.

data_fields.name: Casino
data_fields.description: Name of the state-owned casino facility

data_fields.name: Provider
data_fields.description: Name of the sports wagering service provider/brand

data_fields.name: Settled Wagers
data_fields.description: Total amount of wagers settled during the period (handle)

data_fields.name: Revenues
data_fields.description: Gross gaming revenue (settled wagers minus payouts)

data_fields.name: State Share
data_fields.description: State tax payment (10% of revenues)

KS.ggr_definition: Revenues = Settled Wagers - Payouts to winners
KS.ggr_formula: Revenues = Settled Wagers - Winning Payouts. State Share = Revenues * 10%
KS.promo_deduction: Not explicitly shown in reports. May be factored into revenue calculation.
KS.scraping_approach: PDF download with semi-predictable URL pattern (media IDs are not sequential but filenames are predictable). Each PDF has 2 pages: page 1 = monthly summary, page 2 = fiscal year cumulative. Simple table structure with Casino, Provider, Settled Wagers, Revenues, State Share columns. Can be parsed with tabula.
KS.notes: KS has clean, simple PDF reports. Casino/provider pairing model is unique. 4 state-owned casinos with up to 3 online skins each. Currently 4 retail + 7 online operator entries. Page 1 = monthly data, Page 2 = FY cumulative. Reports marked 'unaudited'. Only 5 data columns - straightforward to parse. 10% flat tax rate for both online and retail.
KS.log: Fetched kslottery.com page - found PDF links organized by year (2023-2026). Downloaded Feb 2026 PDF (2 pages). Page 1 confirmed: Casino, Provider, Settled Wagers, Revenues, State Share columns with Retail/Online subtotals. Page 2 has same structure for FY cumulative. All operator pairings documented.

IA.launch_notes: Iowa sports betting launched August 15, 2019. One of the earlier states to legalize.
IA.primary_format: PDF (image-based, likely scanned - may require OCR)
IA.archive_depth: From August 2019 to present. Archived reports available at separate URL.
IA.requires_js_notes: Direct PDF download links. Page uses standard HTML with media download links.
IA.operator_notes: Page 1: Revenue by CASINO LICENSEE (retail + internet combined per casino). Page 2: Online-only breakdown by OPERATOR ENTITY NAME. 19 casinos on page 1, 13 online operators on page 2.
IA.retail_online_split_notes: Excellent split: each casino shows TOTAL, RETAIL, and INTERNET separately. Page 2 is dedicated online-only operator breakdown.

data_fields_page1.name: Casino Name
data_fields_page1.description: Name of the licensed casino facility

data_fields_page1.name: SPORTS WAGERING PAYOUTS
data_fields_page1.description: Total payouts to winners (retail + internet combined)

data_fields_page1.name: RETAIL NET RECEIPTS
data_fields_page1.description: Net receipts from retail/in-person wagering only

data_fields_page1.name: INTERNET NET RECEIPTS
data_fields_page1.description: Net receipts from internet/online wagering only

data_fields_page1.name: STATE TAX
data_fields_page1.description: State tax payment (6.75% of net receipts)

data_fields_page2.name: Online Operator Name
data_fields_page2.description: Name of the online sports wagering operator entity

data_fields_page2.name: INTERNET NET RECEIPTS
data_fields_page2.description: Net receipts from internet wagering for this operator

IA.ggr_definition: Net Receipts = Handle - Payouts (equivalent to Gross Gaming Revenue)
IA.ggr_formula: Net Receipts = Sports Wagering Handle - Sports Wagering Payouts. State Tax = Net Receipts * 6.75%
IA.promo_deduction: Not explicitly shown in reports. May be factored into net receipts.
IA.scraping_approach: PDFs appear to be IMAGE-BASED (JBIG2 compressed) requiring OCR. The tabular structure is clear but text extraction may fail on standard PDF parsers. Recommend using OCR (Tesseract) or a specialized PDF tool. Two-page reports: page 1 = casino-level breakdown, page 2 = online operator breakdown. 19 casinos across 3 table sections on page 1. Also has FY cumulative reports available.
IA.notes: Iowa has the MOST DETAILED data of the Tier 3 states: 19 casino-level breakdowns with retail/internet split for each, PLUS 13 online operator-level breakdown on page 2. However, PDFs are IMAGE-BASED requiring OCR which significantly increases scraping difficulty. Data goes back to Aug 2019 (one of the longest histories). 6.75% is one of the lowest tax rates. FY cumulative reports also available. Net receipts can be negative for individual casinos/operators in a given month.
IA.log: Fetched irgc.iowa.gov page - found monthly and FY report links with media IDs. Downloaded Feb 2026 monthly and FY2026 cumulative PDFs. PDF viewer confirmed 2-page structure: page 1 = 19 casinos with 10 data rows each (total/retail/internet x handle/payouts/net receipts + tax), page 2 = 13 online operators with 3 rows each. PDF is image-based (JBIG2 encoding) requiring OCR for text extraction.

scraping_difficulty_ranking.reason: CSV API from data.ct.gov - no parsing needed
CSV files available, minimal data (4 fields)
Excel files available, rich data, non-predictable URLs
Image-based PDFs requiring OCR, complex multi-table layout

key_findings.unique_tax_model: TN taxes handle (1.85%) not GGR - only US state to do this
key_findings.best_data_quality: IA has most granular data (19 casinos x retail/internet split + 13 online operators), LA has sport-by-sport breakdown
key_findings.operator_pairing_models: KS (casino/provider) and KY (venue/service provider) use unique pairing models
key_findings.tribal_compact: CT operates under tribal compacts with Mashantucket Pequot and Mohegan tribes
key_findings.parish_model: LA is the only state with parish-by-parish voter approval (55 of 64 parishes)
key_findings.tax_rate_range: 6.75% (IA) to 21.5% (LA online) - TN's 1.85% handle tax is not directly comparable
